Michel Fortin Crib Notes

Michel Fortin Crib Notes
www.halbertology.com 2
Michel Fortin Crib Notes
Michel Fortin, $100 Million Man – 24 June, 2015
Copy is just a written form of a good sales presentation. Gary Halbert said it best: a copywriter
is a salesman in print. The most successful copywriters are face-to-face salesmen first.
Gary was the master of storytelling, and that’s what made him such a great copywriter. He
always made his stories make sense in the end, even if they had tangents in the beginning. And
they were rehearsed, even if they seemed natural. Good stories are something that evolve:
over time, you know what details to gloss over and what parts to play up. People love closure,
so leave them on a cliffhanger at the end of a letter so they have to read the next one.
If you have the capability to think with an incredible amount of empathy, you can visualize how
people will react to your story, how they’ll take action based on what you’re saying. Get the
reader involved in the story. Recommended reading: Stephen King’s
On Writing, Eugene
Schwartz’s
Breakthrough Advertising.
We may be in different industries, but we’re all humans born with the same disease: emotions.
Everybody makes decisions based on emotion, so you need to know how to tweak that.
Immerse yourself in your industry, know it inside and out: your writing will be better if you have
a personal connection to the topic. Copywriting is 80% research, 20% writing copy.
Spends more time testing funnels and other lead generation processes (specifically for social
media) than on copy itself these days. The medium of copy is evolving, these days multi-media
is quite important, if not more important. There is a danger of a disconnect if you try and use
all these different mediums, but so long as you remain true to salesmanship and what you’re
about, you should still do well.
How do you find the story when there doesn’t seem to be one? The more market research you
do, the more ideas will come to you. When in doubt, talk to happy clients and try to get them
to sell
you on the product (or a competing product). Don’t be afraid to dig deeper.
If you can’t find a unique sales proposition, make one up! Don’t lie, but dig into the product and
the life of the product’s creator (e.g., golf ball-sized tumor removed, secret revealed!). Look at
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 2 of 5 --

Michel Fortin Crib Notes
www.halbertology.com 3
it from a different angle if you need to. Michel has changed a product in the past so he could
put something in the copy.
Are your leads ready to take an OATH?
 Oblivious – They don’t know they have a problem. Educate on problem.
 Apathetic – They know they have a problem, but don’t care. Educate on why it’s a
problem.
 Thinking – They’re shopping around for something that will solve their problem.
Educate them on why your product is best.
 Hurting – They know they need your product. Educate on the urgency.
Fortin-ize your copy! The “what” is more important than the “how.” Sometimes you need to
change just one little thing to make your copy great, according to his observations in critiquing
other people’s copy.
Michel uses a formula based on AIDA when writing copy, QUEST:
 Qualify – Narrow your market to those who would be interested in the product. (“Have
wrinkles?” vs. “Suffering from wrinkles?”)
 Understand – You have to write it like you’re a blessing to the reader’s life at that
moment, that you understand their problem (empathize) and have the perfect solution.
 Educate – Describe your product, benefits and features, why it’s the best solution for
their problem.
 Stimulate – Build value, add testimonials, talk about the price value. Get people
excited about your offer!
 Transition – Turn them from a browser to a buyer. This isn’t “closing” – it’s not the end
of your relationship! You can increase sales dramatically in this stage by avoiding speed
bumps and continuing to educate even at the order form.
When you’re passionate about it, it just comes out! Hone it, and learn sales in order to learn
copy. Being zealous about things is part of salesmanship, but the biggest hurdle is putting that
on paper. As Peter Stone said, “Write fearlessly, edit ruthlessly.” Don’t stop mid-way, it can
stifle the creative process. Sometimes dictating is the best way to write copy.
The power of metaphors, and thinking in relative terms, is the most important lesson from Gary
Halbert, per Michel. If you have trouble, try to look at it from a scientific standpoint and it’s a
little easier: go to the end and then come back. What story will relate your point to the client?
UPWORDS: Universal Picture Words Or Relatable Descriptive Sentences
How to write a good stick letter: 1) Educate them on how to consume the product, 2) Reinforce
your support and show them where to get that support if they need it, 3) Add an extra bonus
that they didn’t expect, reinforce the “gift” mentality.
Perfect Prospect Profile: tell a story of a day in the life of your ideal customer (psychographics,
demographics, geographics, technographics). If you’re reading something on your phone vs. in
a newspaper, those are two very different states o f mind. In order to walk a mile in someone
else’s shoes, you need to first take off your own.
www.getwsodownload.com

websites apply hereto and must be read herewith.

any communication from or between us, are for general information and educational purposes only. Certain
speakers and persons due to educate may or may not in some instances be able to do so in part or at all. You
acknowledge this and agree that no claim at all may be made against any such person or ourselves or our
partners affiliates agents and employees or any person in this regard.

accuracy, completeness or suitability of the information and materials found or offered on our websites
and/or materials and/or service for any particular purpose. You acknowledge that such information and
materials may contain errors or inaccuracies and we expressly exclude liability for any such inaccuracies or
errors to the fullest extent permitted by law.
We have made every effort to accurately represent this product and/or service offered and its potential.
Despite this there is no guarantee that you will earn any money using the techniques and ideas in these
materials or as a result of the services. Examples in materials are not to be interpreted as a promise or
guarantee of earnings. Earning potential is entirely dependent on the person using our product, ideas,
techniques and services. We do not purport this as a get rich quick scheme – the techniques may be proven
however their capacity to create wealth is based purely on the commitment, determination and willingness of
the individual(s) concerned.
Materials in our product and/or our website and/or services may contain information that includes, or is
based upon, forward-looking statements within the meaning of the securities litigation reform act of 1995.
Forward-looking statements give our expectations or forecasts of future events and are intended to express
our opinion of earnings potential. You can identify these statements by the fact that they do not relate strictly
to historical or current facts. Words such as “anticipate,” “project,” “estimate,” “expect,” “plan,” “intend,”
“believe,” and other words and terms of similar meaning in connection with a description of potential
earnings or financial performance might be used.
There are many factors that will be important in determining your actual results and no guarantees are made
that you will achieve results similar to ours or anybody else’s. In fact no guarantees are made that you will
achieve any results from our ideas and techniques in our material. All and earnings or income examples
provided are only estimates off what we think you could earn. At no time is there any assurance that you will
in fact also do so by replying on these figures.. We do not purport that any prior success or past results as to
income earnings are indicative of your future success or results.
Internet businesses and marketing and earnings derived there from may have unknown risks attached and
are not suitable for everyone and there is risk that one could experience significant losses or make no money
at all.
Business carries a high level of risk, and may not be suitable for all investors. Before deciding to set out on
any business venture, you should carefully consider your investment objectives, level of experience, and risk
appetite. The possibility exists that you could sustain a loss of some or all of your initial investment and
therefore, you should not invest money that you cannot afford to lose. You should be aware of all the risks
associated with a business, and seek advice if you have any doubts from an independent financial advisor
and/or qualified professionals such as lawyer or accountant before acting on any of this information.
Users of our services and/or products are advised to do their own due diligence when making business
decisions or any reliance made based on them. We are not to be held liable for the successor failure of your
decisions relating to any information or product or services presented by us to you and relied on or used by
you.

communications and agree to hold any such information of any sort confidential at all times.

offense.
We would never mean to say or pretend that any person will or is likely to achieve profits or loses similar to
those discussed on this website. Each person may have different results. In fact, it’s pretty certain they and
you will not have similar results and there are frequently sharp differences between hypothetical
performance results and actual results subsequently achieved by any particular business. So the saying "Past
performance is not indicative of future results" really holds true here.
One of the limitations of hypothetical performance results is that they are generally prepared with the benefit
of hindsight. Also, hypothetical trading does not involve financial risk and that’s why no hypothetical profit
record can completely account for the impact of financial risk in business.
There are a lot of other factors related to the market in general or to the implementation of any specific
business objective which cannot be fully accounted for in the preparation of hypothetical performance results
and all of which can adversely affect results.
Although we make every attempt to assure accuracy, we do not give any express or implied warranty as to its
accuracy. What we are saying here is that we do not accept any liability for any error or omission. You'll see
we have examples posted now and again. These are for illustrative purposes only so please do not be
construe them as investment advice or strategy.

representatives including product developers and authors and guest speakers do not and cannot give
investment advice or invite customers to engage in investments through this website.
